conditions: Chronic low back pain (degenerative disc disease)
health.mental_health: Since being laid off from a long-term job, Jordan has had bouts of insomnia and anxiety about finances and ageism in hiring; they are working with their family doctor on stress management and are considering short-term counselling through an employee assistance-style community program.
recent_visits: Family doctor visit for A1C review and medication adjustment (last month)
DEXA scan follow-up and osteoporosis management discussion (3 months ago)
Physiotherapy assessment for low back pain (recent)
Pharmacy medication review focusing on fall risk and bone health (recent)

finance.debt: Carrying a remaining mortgage on a Toronto condo and a small line of credit used during the layoff gap; Jordan is also repaying a short-term balance transfer they used to bridge benefits before starting the new role.
investments: Defined contribution pension from prior employer (locked-in account)
recent_transactions: Paid $1,240 to condo maintenance fees and utilities
Transferred $600 to pay down line of credit used during layoff
Purchased a $185 glucose monitor sensor refill and test strips
Paid $320 for physiotherapy sessions not fully covered
Deposited first full paycheque from current warehouse role
Booked $1,050 for a professional certification course (forklift/health & safety leadership refresh)

relationships: Partner: Avery Chen (living together in Toronto; supportive but worried about Jordan overworking to prove themselves after the layoff)
Adult child: Priya Desai (lives in Mississauga; helps with tech and job-search paperwork, relationship occasionally strained around retirement planning)
Sibling: Rohan Desai (in Calgary; sporadic contact, disagreements about supporting an aging aunt)
Close friend: Marta Kowalski (former coworker; shares leads and references from the old job)
Neighbour: Samira Al-Hassan (checks in on Jordan after a minor slip/fall last winter)
social.social_media_activity: Low public footprint; Jordan uses LinkedIn to connect with logistics managers and post occasional industry articles, keeps Facebook private for family updates, and avoids posting about health or finances.
private_conversations: Discussing the layoff circumstances and whether it was age-related discrimination
Talking with Avery about fears of not being able to retire in Toronto
Confiding in Marta about managing diabetes at work and needing predictable breaks
Discussing gender identity boundaries at work and how to correct pronouns without conflict

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri, 7:00 a.m.–3:30 p.m.; occasional Saturday overtime during peak shipping weeks
regular_appointments: Monthly A1C/diabetes check-ins or lab requisitions as scheduled by family doctor
Pharmacy pickup and medication synchronization every 4 weeks
Strength/balance class at a community centre twice weekly (bone health focus)
upcoming_events: Workplace health & safety audit walkthrough (in two weeks)
Dinner with Priya in Mississauga to review retirement and benefits paperwork (this weekend)
DEXA scan recheck scheduled for later this year
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 5:30 a.m. for breakfast and glucose check, TTC commute to the warehouse, walks the floor and checks receiving/putaway metrics, takes a mid-morning snack to prevent lows, home by early evening for a short walk and meal planning; weekends are used for errands, physio exercises, and catching up on job-transition paperwork.

professional.company: Mid-sized third-party logistics (3PL) provider serving e-commerce and retail clients in the GTA (Etobicoke distribution hub)
projects: Reducing pick/pack error rate through updated SOPs and refresher training
Implementing a new WMS module for cycle counting and location accuracy
Developing a joint health-and-safety plan to reduce slips/trips and manual handling injuries
Reworking shift handoff notes to improve on-time outbound performance
professional.performance_issues: Jordan is still learning the new WMS and client SLAs; they worry about being judged for asking questions and sometimes stay late to double-check reports, which adds fatigue and aggravates back pain.
workplace_relationships: Direct report lead hand, Miguel Santos, is supportive but pushes for faster changes than Jordan prefers
The site director, Karen O'Neill, expects quick KPI improvements and has limited patience for onboarding needs
HR contact, Nisha Patel, has been helpful with benefits enrollment and accommodation questions

hobbies: Lakefront walking (Harbourfront and Martin Goodman Trail)
Cooking Indian and Mediterranean meals adapted for diabetes-friendly carbs
Gardening on the condo balcony (herbs and tomatoes)
habits: Checks blood glucose most mornings and logs readings
Meal preps on Sundays to keep carbs consistent during workdays
Limits alcohol to occasional social drinks; avoids sugary beverages
Uses a cane on bad back-pain days but avoids bringing it to work out of fear of stigma
Tends to overwork/skip breaks when anxious about proving themselves in the new job
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with partner in a two-bedroom condo near the Bloor–Danforth line; after the layoff, they downsized discretionary spending and started tracking expenses more closely while rebuilding savings.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Diabetes-conscious diet with higher fibre and protein, moderate carbs, reduced sodium for blood pressure, and calcium/vitamin D focus for bone health; prefers home-cooked meals and brings lunch to work to avoid cafeteria fast food.
